REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT GLORIA YAM, CHARLES KIM DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR TRESPASS, VIOLATION OF PRIVACY, INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND ANTICIPATORY BREACH OF CONTRACT. NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO, (1) TENANCY; (2) TENANCY VS. LIFE ESTATE; (3) EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL; (3) CONTRACT WITH CHARLES KIM; (4) RESTORATION OF EVERYTHING OF VALUE TO GLORIA YAM; (5) WRONGFUL USE OR INTENT TO DEFRAUD, ETC. PLAINTIFF MAY, IF PLAINTIFF CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NUISANCE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-31-2018 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Kristin Iverson, (415-788-1900 for Defedant/Moving party Gloria Yam.
No other appearance

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT GLORIA YAM, CHARLES KIM DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action for trespass, violation of privacy, Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress, Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress and anticipatory breach of contract. No opposition filed. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to all other causes of action for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action as to each defendant, including but not limited to, (1) tenancy; (2) tenancy vs. life estate; (3) equitable estoppel; (3) contract with Charles Kim; (4) restoration of everything of value to Gloria Yam; (5) wrongful use or intent to defraud, etc. Plaintiff may, if Plaintiff can do so in good faith, allege a cause of action for nuisance. Order signed in Open Court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
